title: Add input validation to user creation handler
description: A request handler for creating users currently accepts any input without validation. It needs proper input validation for name, email, and age fields.

variant.spec_text: The handler (src/handler.py) has a handle_create_user(request_data: dict) function that creates users from a dict with 'name', 'email', and 'age' fields. Currently it accepts any input without validation. Add input validation with these rules: (1) name must be a string of 1-100 characters containing only alphanumeric characters and spaces, (2) email must be a string containing '@', (3) age must be an integer between 0 and 150 inclusive. On invalid input, return {'status': 400, 'error': '<description>'} instead of creating the user. Valid input should still return {'status': 201, 'user': {...}}. You may use src/validators.py for helper functions or put validation inline.
